title: quercus × fernowii
intro: Quercus × fernowii, also known as the Fernow oak, is a hybrid deciduous tree that results from the cross between Quercus alba (white oak) and Quercus rubra (red oak). The bark of the Quercus × fernowii contains tannins, which are known for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable medicinal plant. The health benefits of Quercus × fernowii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive well-being. Quercus × fernowii can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
